memoryArtifacts.description: Rudolf I (c.?1284 - 2 March 1356), a member of the House of Ascania, was Duke of Saxe-Wittenberg from 1298 until his death. By the Golden Bull of 1356 he was acknowledged as Elector of Saxony and Mars …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg於1284年出生在海因里希_普洛文次 薩辛藍德可萊斯 維騰格時，他的父親Herzog Albrecht von Sachsen-Wittenberg II34歲，母親Gertrud Agnes Gräfin von Habsburg27歲。他於1303年與Jutta von Brandenburg結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1356年3月11日在他的家鄉享年72歲，埋葬在海因里希_普洛文次 薩辛藍德可萊斯 維騰格維騰格。

nameMeanings.name: Herzog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

variants.name: Hartsook
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herceg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Croatian Stanko.

Croatian: nickname from herceg ‘duke’, a title of the medieval feudal lords of Herzegovina (‘duke's land’, a region in Bosnia and Herzegovina), derived from the Hungarian form of German Herzog . Compare Erceg .

variants.name: Herczeg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Imre, Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Ferenc, Gaza, Istvan, Janos.
Hungarian form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herzig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Fritzi, Hans, Horst, Markus, Monika.
German (eastern and central Germany and Silesia, Switzerland): variant of   Herzog  .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed with hard ‘strong, hardy’, for instance Herto.

variants.name: Hertzog
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Herzog and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Hertzog is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Hartzog
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsock
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsoe
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsough
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Rudolf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Latinized version, Rudolphus, of the Germanic name Hrōdwulf ( see Rolf ). It was introduced to the English-speaking world from Germany in the 19th century. Rudolf was a hereditary name among the Habsburgs, the Holy Roman Emperors and rulers of Austria, from the Emperor Rudolf I ( 1218–91 ) to the Archduke Rudolf , Crown Prince of Austria-Hungary, who died in mysterious circumstances at his country house at Meyerling in 1889 . Rudolf Rassendyll was the central character of Anthony Hope's adventure stories The Prisoner of Zenda ( 1894 ) and Rupert of Hentzau ( 1898 ), in which he is an English gentleman who bears a great physical resemblance to the King of Ruritania, to whom he is distantly related. He successfully impersonates the king for reasons of state. In the early 20th century the popularity of this name was further enhanced by the American silent-film actor Rudolph Valentino ( 1895–1926 ), born in Italy as Rodolfo di Valentina d'Antonguolla . It has not been used much in recent times, perhaps being inextricably associated with ‘the red-nosed reindeer’ of the Christmas song.

variants.name: Rodolf
meanings: Variant of   Rudolf .

variants.name: Rudolph
meanings: From a Latinized version, Rudolphus, of the Germanic name Hrōdwulf ( see Rolf ). It was introduced to the English-speaking world from Germany in the 19th century. Rudolf was a hereditary name among the Habsburgs, the Holy Roman Emperors and rulers of Austria, from the Emperor Rudolf I ( 1218–91 ) to the Archduke Rudolf , Crown Prince of Austria-Hungary, who died in mysterious circumstances at his country house at Meyerling in 1889 . Rudolf Rassendyll was the central character of Anthony Hope's adventure stories The Prisoner of Zenda ( 1894 ) and Rupert of Hentzau ( 1898 ), in which he is an English gentleman who bears a great physical resemblance to the King of Ruritania, to whom he is distantly related. He successfully impersonates the king for reasons of state. In the early 20th century the popularity of this name was further enhanced by the American silent-film actor Rudolph Valentino ( 1895–1926 ), born in Italy as Rodolfo di Valentina d'Antonguolla . It has not been used much in recent times, perhaps being inextricably associated with ‘the red-nosed reindeer’ of the Christmas song.

en.lifeSummary: When Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg was born in 1284, in Wittenberg, Province of Saxony, Prussia, his father, Herzog Albrecht von Sachsen-Wittenberg II, was 34 and his mother, Gertrud Agnes Gräfin von Habsburg, was 27. He married Jutta von Brandenburg in 1303. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 11 March 1356, in his hometown, at the age of 72, and was buried in Wittenberg, Wittenberg, Province of Saxony, Prussia.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg est né en 1284, à Landkreis Wittenberg, Provinz Sachsen, Prusse, son père, Herzog Albrecht von Sachsen-Wittenberg II, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Gertrud Agnes Gräfin von Habsburg, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Jutta von Brandenburg en 1303. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 11 mars 1356, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 72 ans, et a été enterré à Wittenberg, Landkreis Wittenberg, Provinz Sachsen, Prusse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg 1284 in Landkreis Wittenberg, Provinz Sachsen, Preußen geboren wurde, war sein Vater Herzog Albrecht von Sachsen-Wittenberg II 34 und seine Mutter Gertrud Agnes Gräfin von Habsburg 27. Er hat 1303 Jutta von Brandenburg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 11. März 1356 im Alter von 72 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Wittenberg, Landkreis Wittenberg, Provinz Sachsen, Preußen begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg è nato il nel 1284 nel Landkreis Wittenberg, Provinz Sachsen, Preußen, il suo padre, Herzog Albrecht von Sachsen-Wittenberg II, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Gertrud Agnes Gräfin von Habsburg, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Jutta von Brandenburg nel 1303. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 11 marzo 1356, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 72 anni, e fu sepolto a Wittenberg, Landkreis Wittenberg, Provinz Sachsen, Preußen.

ko.Wittenberg, Saxony, Germany: 독일 플레우ss 플로빈스 프 사스니 랜드크레이스 윗텐버그
ko.lifeSummary: Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg(이)가 1284년 플레우ss 플로빈츠 작센 랜드크레이스 윗텐버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Herzog Albrecht von Sachsen-Wittenberg II(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Gertrud Agnes Gräfin von Habsburg(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1303년 Jutta von Brandenburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1356년 3월 11일 그의 고향에서 72세의 나이로 사망했으며 플레우ss 플로빈츠 작센 랜드크레이스 윗텐버그 윗텐버그에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg nasceu em 1284, em Landkreis Wittenberg, Provinz Sachsen, Preußen, seu pai, Herzog Albrecht von Sachsen-Wittenberg II, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Gertrud Agnes Gräfin von Habsburg, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Jutta von Brandenburg em 1303. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 11 de março de 1356, em sua cidade natal, com 72 anos, e foi sepultado em Wittenberg, Landkreis Wittenberg, Provinz Sachsen, Preußen.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg родился в 1284 г. в местности ландкрис виттенбирг, провинз сачсен, Пруссия, его отцу по имени Herzog Albrecht von Sachsen-Wittenberg II было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Gertrud Agnes Gräfin von Habsburg, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Jutta von Brandenburg в 1303 г. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 11 марта 1356 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 72 лет и был похоронен в местности виттенбирг, ландкрис виттенбирг, провинз сачсен, Пруссия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rudolf I. Herzog von Sachsen-Wittenberg nació en 1284, en Landkreis Wittenberg, Provinz Sachsen, Preußen, su papá, Herzog Albrecht von Sachsen-Wittenberg II, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Gertrud Agnes Gräfin von Habsburg, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Jutta von Brandenburg en 1303. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 11 de marzo de 1356, en su pueblo, a la edad de 72 años, y fue sepultado en Wittenberg, Landkreis Wittenberg, Provinz Sachsen, Preußen.
